# Closure Research Initiative

> Foundational mathematics and structural physics research program studying closed physical theory from primitive comparison data.

## Canonical Site

- Site: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/
- Director and author: Chast K. Wolfe
- ORCID: https://orcid.org/0009-0008-8846-2539
- Contact: chast.wolfe@closureresearchinitiative.org
- GitHub discussions: https://github.com/CKW25/closure-research-initiative/discussions
- Sitemap: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/sitemap.xml
- Feed: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/feed.xml
- License: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/license/
- Sources and citation: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/sources/
- External sources BibTeX: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/external-sources.bib

## Disambiguation

Closure Research Initiative is not about mine closure, industrial site closure, clinical-trial closure, or institutional research shutdowns. Here "closure" means mathematical and physical self-containment: a closed physical theory may not take an external observer, background manifold, pre-supplied state space, gauge convention, causal order, or field content as primitive.

## Core Idea

The formal development starts from intrinsic comparison data. A comparison is a relation among states, invariant under the symmetries of the comparison world it helps constitute, not an externally imposed label. Under stated structural hypotheses, the theory develops rectangular completeness, quotient semantics, subsystem-attribution obstruction, transport obstruction, curvature at the faithful smooth-realization stage, and a rigidity result for genuinely closed realized three-dimensional systems. As of monograph v5, the smooth-realization faithfulness condition is decomposed into a torsion condition (T) and an intrinsic detectability axiom (D), shown exactly necessary by ghost counter-models (torsion classes and profinite realizations), and discharged on a canonical realization: a structure trichotomy (Gleason-Yamabe, van Dantzig) shows every scale-compatible completion of the transport law is Lie, totally disconnected, or mixed, axiom (D) selects the Lie branch, and under (T) the Malcev completion of the two-step transport quotient realizes the carrier with an injective second-jet comparison map by construction.

The Foundations page includes a compactification-orientation note: the topological fact R^3 union a point at infinity is homeomorphic to S^3 is useful intuition, but it is not the proof of the S3 theorem. The theorem-level mechanism is the CFSG chain from closed-system admissibility to the action groupoid, isometric transport, frame-bundle transitivity, constant sectional curvature, and the simply connected compact space-form case.

The Overview page frames QFT, GR, and standard cosmology as recovery targets rather than opponents. Their empirical success constrains the closure program: the question is whether their standard inputs--background spacetime, field content, regulators, renormalization scales, boundary conditions, and cosmological initial data--are internally recoverable and which remain bridge assumptions. Renormalization is treated as a precise framework, compact S3 is not claimed to remove local UV divergences, and no cosmological bounce theorem is claimed without a separate dynamical result. The Overview also includes a standard-FLRW recovery-target figure: the flat R3 transverse-distance law D_M = D_C is compared with the closed S3 law D_M = R_c sin(D_C/R_c), emphasizing that a large-radius closed realization locally reproduces the flat approximation while closing globally.

The Structural Map includes several neighboring programs. Rovelli's partial-observables work and Dittrich's partial/complete-observables program are cited as adjacent external literature on observability without an external clock or frame. Abramsky and Brandenburger's sheaf-theoretic contextuality is cited as a mathematical neighbor where local compatibility can fail to extend to a global section. Spekkens, Wolfe, Schmid, Wood, and collaborators are cited as adjacent literature on operational equivalence, causal assumptions, and when observed correlations justify representational distinctions. The site does not claim that this literature proves closure or endorses CRI; the comparison is methodological.

## Claim Support

- Foundational comparison data, intrinsicality, rectangular completeness, quotient semantics, transport obstruction, interface forcing (conditions (T)/(D), structure trichotomy, canonical realization), and the broad theorem chain: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/csm/
- Finitary two-subsystem comparison worlds and the subsystem-attribution obstruction: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/ccw/
- Conditional S3 theorem at the faithful smooth-realization/manifold stage: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/cfsg/
- Compactification intuition versus the formal S3 rigidity proof: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/idea/#compactification-rigidity
- Public charge-sector consequence and experimental interface: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/predictions/
- Canonical citation targets and external works cited for background, comparison, experimental, and legal context: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/sources/

## Best Entry Points

- Homepage: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/
- Foundational orientation: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/idea/
- Technical overview without proofs: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/overview/
- Logical map and comparison to neighboring programs: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/map/
- Minimal worked example: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/example/
- Notation and definitions: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/notation/
- Objections and remaining interface conditions: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/objections/
- Reading guide: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/guide/
- Preprints and downloads: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/preprints/
- Research notes: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/notes/
- Corpus Query: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/ask/
- Public prediction page: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/predictions/
- Sources and citation hub: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/sources/

## Public Works

- Closed Systems from Comparison Completeness: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/csm/
  - Current release: v5, June 11, 2026; DOI pending.
  - PDF: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/csm.pdf
  - Archived DOI version: v3, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20589038
  - Superseded archived PDFs: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/archive/csm/csm-v4.pdf, https://closureresearchinitiative.org/archive/csm/csm-v3.pdf, https://closureresearchinitiative.org/archive/csm/csm-v2.pdf, and https://closureresearchinitiative.org/archive/csm/csm-v1.pdf

- Closed Comparison Worlds and the Obstruction to Subsystem Attribution: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/ccw/
  - Current release: v2, June 10, 2026; DOI pending.
  - Archived DOI version: v1, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20600752
  - PDF: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/ccw.pdf

- Closure Forces Spherical Geometry: Genuinely Closed Three-Dimensional Systems Are Diffeomorphic to S3: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/cfsg/
  - Current release: v2, June 10, 2026; DOI pending.
  - PDF: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/cfsg.pdf

- Structural Closure and the Cosmological Misnomer: Admissibility, Expansion, and the Geometry of Closed Systems: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/scc/
  - Current release: v2, June 10, 2026; DOI pending.
  - PDF: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/scc.pdf

- Rectangular Completeness Encompasses Standard Physical Closure: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/rc/
  - Current release: v2, June 10, 2026; DOI pending.
  - PDF: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/rc.pdf

- Foundational Closure and Primitive Structural Input: A Four-Axis Taxonomy: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/fe/
  - Current release: v2, June 10, 2026; DOI pending.
  - PDF: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/fe.pdf

- A Factorization Criterion for Route Invariants with Fixed Endpoint Data: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/rie/
  - Current release: v2, June 10, 2026; DOI pending.
  - Archived DOI version: v1, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20617818
  - PDF: https://closureresearchinitiative.org/rie.pdf

## Public Prediction

The current public empirical prediction is a charge-sector exclusion. Conditional on the physically realized closed electromagnetic phase sector constructed in Chapter 18 of the monograph, every free/asymptotic particle with true vacuum electromagnetic charge must lie in the denominator-3 lattice. See https://closureresearchinitiative.org/predictions/.

## Citation Guidance

Use the current-version page URL and BibTeX block for current releases. Use archived DOI links only for the version to which the DOI was assigned. The Sources and Citation page collects the canonical citation targets for the monograph, public preprints, author ORCID, and outside works cited across the site. The external-source BibTeX download is available at https://closureresearchinitiative.org/external-sources.bib.

## Corpus Assistant

Corpus Query is a public bounded scholarly query interface at https://closureresearchinitiative.org/ask/. Its retrieval corpus is generated from the monograph, six public preprints, website pages, llms.txt, and external-source BibTeX. It supports guide, locate, and cite modes. Its answers are instructed to use only retrieved corpus excerpts and cite those excerpts by source label; short in-session history is used only to interpret follow-up queries.

## Crawl Guidance

The site is public and crawlable. The robots file allows all crawlers and points to the sitemap. Download links under /dl/ are Cloudflare Worker routes that increment download counts and redirect to the corresponding public PDF, ZIP, image, or archive file.
